lineage_provenance.raw: cherry zashimi thca flower 14g click here for coa cherry zashimi is a pretty rad strain if you're into flavorful and potent buds. it’s a hybrid that typically blends cherry pie with zkittlez and it’s got a lot to offer. first up the flavor and aroma are killer. imagine a sweet fruity cherry flavor with a hint of candy like sweetness mixed in. it’s got a really nice smooth taste that makes smoking it a pleasure. the smell is similarly fruity with a touch of earthiness giving you that classic sweet and sour vibe. when you smoke cherry zashimi you’re in for a treat. the high usually kicks off with a burst of euphoria and energy making it great for social hangouts or creative sessions. you’ll feel uplifted and happy which is perfect if you’re looking to enhance your mood. after a while it transitions into a more relaxed mellow state so you’re not left feeling too buzzed or jittery. it’s a nice balance that makes it versatile for different times of the day. in terms of effects cherry zashimi is known for giving you a good mix of mental stimulation and body relaxation. it’s not too heavy so you won’t get couch locked but it still helps you unwind. plus it can be a nice option if you’re looking to spark up some appetite or just chill after a long day. overall cherry zashimi is a solid choice if you want a flavorful strain that gives you a happy balanced high with a sweet twist. terpenes myrcene this is usually the dominant terpene in cherry zashimi. it has a sweet earthy and musky aroma often with a hint of fruitiness. myrcene is known for its relaxing and sedative effects which contribute to the mellow part of the high. it can also enhance the overall experience by providing a soothing body buzz that balances the uplifting head high. limonene this terpene brings a citrusy lemon like scent and flavor. limonene is known for its mood enhancing and stress relieving properties. it adds a bright uplifting element to the high making it feel more euphoric and energizing. it also contributes to the sweet fruity flavor profile of cherry zashimi. caryophyllene with its spicy peppery aroma caryophyllene adds a bit of kick to the strain’s flavor. it’s unique because it can interact with the body’s cannabinoid receptors providing potential anti inflammatory and pain relieving benefits. in cherry zashimi caryophyllene complements the other terpenes by adding complexity to the f
